name: Col d'Aspin and Col de Coupe Loop
name_original: Le col d'Aspin à partir de Campan
description: Cycle the difficult Col d'Aspin from Campan, a 45.4-mile route with 5872 feet of gain, offering stunning Pyrenean views and a quiet ride.
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Campan are set within the Hautes-Pyrénées, characterized by diverse Pyrenean landscapes. The region features challenging mountain passes, serene valleys, and high-altitude lakes, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Riders can expect significant elevation changes and scenic backdrops, including notable lakes like Lac de Payolle and Lac d'Oncet. The area's geography offers a range of experiences from demanding ascents to exhilarating descents. Best touring cycling routes around Campan The most popular touring cycling route is Col d'Aspin and Col de Coupe Loop, a 45.4 miles (73.1 km) trail that takes 5 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through classic Pyrenean passes. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Auzan Pass – Col d'Aspin loop from Gripp, a difficult 40.8 miles (65.7 km) path. This route offers expansive mountain views and challenging climbs, often passing through picturesque valleys. Local touring cyclists also love the Barèges Village – Scenic Road Through Campan loop from Sainte-Marie de Campan, a 64.9 miles (104.4 km) trail leading through scenic mountain roads, often completed in about 7 hours 29 minutes. Touring cycling around Campan is defined by challenging mountain passes, lush valleys, and high-altitude lakes. The network of routes offers options for various ability levels, from demanding climbs to more moderate scenic rides. The routes in Campan are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 5,500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Campan's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Campan?
A: Campan offers a vast network of touring cycling routes, with over 50 routes detailed on komoot. The broader region boasts more than 400 cycling routes and bike trails, catering to various skill levels and preferences.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Campan?
A: The terrain around Campan is highly diverse, ranging from lush green forests and serene valleys to dramatic mountain vistas and high-altitude lakes. You'll encounter challenging ascents and exhilarating descents through the Pyrenees, with routes featuring both paved surfaces and some unpaved sections for those seeking more rugged adventures.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or less experienced touring cyclists?
A: While Campan is known for its challenging mountain passes, there are options for various ability levels. Out of the routes on komoot, 5 are rated as easy and 12 as moderate, offering less demanding scenic rides through valleys and picturesque areas. These routes provide a great way to experience the region's beauty without tackling the most strenuous climbs.

Q: What makes Campan a popular destination for touring cyclists?
A: Campan's prime location in the Hautes-Pyrénées, its stunning Pyrenean landscapes, and its deep connection to the prestigious Tour de France make it an exceptional destination. Cyclists are drawn to its challenging mountain passes like Col du Tourmalet and Col d'Aspin, diverse natural beauty, and extensive network of routes.

Q: What do other cyclists say about the touring routes in Campan?
A: The touring cycling routes in Campan are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 70 reviews. More than 5,500 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the challenging climbs, expansive mountain views, and the beauty of the Pyrenean passes.

Q: Are there any challenging climbs for experienced touring cyclists?
A: Absolutely. Campan is an excellent base for tackling iconic climbs frequently featured in the Tour de France. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868950195/col-d-aspin-and-col-de-coupe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aspin and Col de Coupe Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24673157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Auzan Pass – Col d'Aspin loop from Gripp&lt;/a&gt; offer significant elevation gain and challenging ascents. The region is also home to the legendary Col du Tourmalet and Hourquette d'Ancizan, providing serious tests for advanced riders.

Q: Can I find circular touring cycling routes in the Campan area?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Campan are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e868950195/col-d-aspin-and-col-de-coupe-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aspin and Col de Coupe Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 73 km, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/41699864" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac de Payolle – Hourquette d'Ancizan loop from La Séoube&lt;/a&gt;, offering a shorter but still challenging circular ride.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Campan offer breathtaking natural beauty. You can expect stunning Pyrenean views, especially from mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/128884" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Col d'Aspin&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/568136" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hourquette d'Ancizan&lt;/a&gt;. The region is also dotted with picturesque lakes such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3755161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lake Orédon&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627219" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lac d'Aubert&lt;/a&gt;, and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/609898" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Laquettes&lt;/a&gt;, which provide scenic resting points and photo opportunities.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling routes near Campan?
A: For those seeking longer rides, Campan serves as an excellent starting point. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/37657053" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barèges Village – Scenic Road Through Campan loop from Sainte-Marie de Campan&lt;/a&gt; is a significant route covering over 104 km, leading through scenic mountain roads and offering an extended touring experience. Many routes can also be combined to create multi-day adventures.

Q: Can I cycle to famous Tour de France climbs from Campan?
A: Yes, Campan is ideally situated for accessing some of the most famous Tour de France climbs. The eastern ascent of the Col du Tourmalet, for instance, starts from Sainte-Marie-de-Campan, offering a challenging 17.2 km climb. The Col d'Aspin and Hourquette d'Ancizan are also easily accessible, providing routes through stunning mountain scenery with a rich cycling heritage.

Q: Are there routes that offer views of Pic du Midi?
A: Yes, some routes provide excellent views of the iconic Pic du Midi. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15023046" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pic du Midi – Tourmalet Pass loop from Gîte du Lièvre&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that offers panoramic vistas, including the majestic Pic du Midi, as you navigate the Pyrenean landscape.

Q: What is the best season for touring cycling in Campan?
A: The best season for touring cycling in Campan is typically from late spring to early autumn (May to October). During these months, the weather is generally more stable, and the high mountain passes are clear of snow, offering optimal conditions for tackling the challenging climbs and enjoying the scenic routes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to cycle the Col d'Aspin from Campan?
faqs.answer: The full route, including the ascent and descent, is approximately 73 kilometers (45 miles) and typically takes around 5 hours and 38 minutes to complete by touring bicycle. This duration can vary based on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the scenery.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as difficult. While the initial section from Campan is gentle, the gradient increases significantly in the final 5-6 kilometers, averaging around 8% and reaching up to 9-10% in some parts. It's considered a rewarding challenge, especially for those new to mountain climbing, offering an iconic experience without being overly daunting.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain and scenery can I expect on the climb?
faqs.answer: Much of the climb from Campan winds through pleasant pine forests, offering a peaceful environment. As you approach the summit of Col d'Aspin, the scenery opens up dramatically, providing expansive views of the high peaks bordering France and Spain, and vistas down into the valleys. You might also encounter free-roaming animals like cows and sheep in this pastoral zone.

faqs.question: What are the best times of year to cycle the Col d'Aspin?
faqs.answer: The Col d'Aspin is accessible for much of the year. However, the best times are typically late spring, summer, and early autumn when the weather is most favorable and the pass is guaranteed to be clear of snow. Heavy snowfall can lead to short-term winter closures.

faqs.question: Are there any specific regulations for cycling within the Parc National des Pyrénées?
faqs.answer: The Col d'Aspin route is located near the Parc National des Pyrénées. While there are no specific entrance fees for cycling on the main roads, it's important to respect the park's regulations regarding wildlife, waste disposal, and general conduct to preserve the natural environment. Always stay on marked paths and roads.

faqs.question: Is the Col d'Aspin from Campan route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route, dogs are generally permitted if they are safely secured (e.g., in a bicycle trailer or basket). However, if you plan to venture off the main road into the surrounding natural areas, especially within the Parc National des Pyrénées, specific regulations regarding dogs (often requiring them to be on a leash or restricting access to certain zones) may apply. Always check local signage.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route in Campan?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Campan is the starting point for this ascent. You can typically find parking in or around the village center. Look for public parking lots or designated street parking areas before beginning your ride.

faqs.question: What notable landmarks or points of interest will I encounter?
faqs.answer: The primary highlight is reaching the summit of Col d'Aspin itself, which offers stunning panoramic views. The route also passes near Col de Coupe. The Col d'Aspin is historically significant, having been featured in the Tour de France over 70 times.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this cycling route overlap with or run alongside several official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Voie d'Aure, Boucle d'Espiadet, and Chemin du Piémont Pyrénéen, among others. These are primarily hiking or multi-use paths, so cyclists should be mindful of other users.

faqs.question: Are there any tips for cyclists tackling the Col d'Aspin from Campan?
faqs.answer: The Campan side of the Col d'Aspin is generally less busy than other prominent Pyrenean passes, offering a quieter riding experience. Kilometer markers along the route provide useful information on the distance to the summit, current height, and average gradient for the upcoming kilometer, helping you pace yourself effectively. It's often used as a warm-up for more challenging climbs like the Col du Tourmalet.
